channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Fortnite v40.20 April 16 Update—Complete Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve come for all the Fortnite v40.20 patch notes in the April 16 update, you’re in the right place. We’ve got news of gameplay fixes, visual improvements, and changes to the Battle Pass and Quests.

As with every week, everyone was waiting for the Fortnite Servers to be back up after the April 16 downtime. While you were doing this, Epic was making some adjustments to the game, which should improve the experience overall.

All Fortnite v40.20 Patch Notes For April 16 Update

The initial patch notes were leaked courtesy of FireMonkey, and here’s what has been reported.

“GENERAL FIXES & IMPROVEMENTS

This update comes with a large number of bug fixes across all of Fortnite. Here are a few notable ones we’ve seen reported in community spaces:

Gameplay

  • Chaos Reloader Shotgun now reloads correctly while unequipped, displays reload progress in the Quick Bar, and fires consistently. 
  • Thorne’s Vampiric Blade no longer causes the jump input to become unresponsive after using its secondary attack.
  • Port-a-Bunkers and Shield Bubbles are now available in Ranked Zero Build as intended.
  • Your arrow will no longer incorrectly appear below teammates’ arrows on the minimap.
  • Returning to Fortnite after Alt-Tabbing or pressing the Windows key no longer incorrectly opens your inventory when in a match. 

Cosmetics & Visuals

  • Fixed an issue causing some Outfits and cosmetics to appear shinier or darker than intended on higher quality settings.
  • Fixed visual and animation physics issues affecting certain Outfits on Performance Mode or lower-end devices.
  • The Saitama Outfit’s cape now loads its textures correctly.
  • The Bugs Bunny Outfit’s eyes no longer appear broken when previewing Pickaxes and Back Blings in the Locker.
  • Darkfire Bundle Weapon Wraps now display their correct individual glow colors instead of all appearing purple.
  • Fixed an issue where some players’ profiles could incorrectly display the default Outfit.
  • Cosmetic previews now display cleanly without a persisting loading spinner in the Shop and Locker.

Battle Pass & Quests

  • Fixed an issue where the Battle Pass could show an incorrect unlock notification when previewing a different reward set. 
  • Daily Quests now display the correct refresh time and no longer reset earlier than stated.

Fortnite OG

  • Item icons no longer appear stretched on Vending Machines.
  • Players can now manage their Quick Bar inventory without issues after using a Pirate Cannon.
  • The Reboot Van at Paradise Palms is no longer missing.
  • Daily Quests no longer reference locations that have been removed from the current map rotation.

Other Modes & Platforms

  • [Reload] Ranked matches no longer display the “Placeholder Playlist” text on the Starter Island.
  • [LEGO Fortnite] Fixed an issue where the World Restore option would give an error when attempting to restore to a previous checkpoint.
  • [Mobile] Fixed visual issues affecting gameplay for some players on Google Pixel 10 devices.
  • [Mobile] The Emote Wheel is no longer too small and difficult to read on certain devices.
  • For Save the World fixes and improvements, check out this Communities Post.

We’re continuing to monitor your reports on social media and in community spaces, please keep them coming and we’ll work to fix them.”

What do you make of the newest Fortnite update? Is there anything Epic keeps overlooking each week? Let us know your gripes and feedback through the Insider Gaming Discord.

We have much more Fortnite news here, including word on a Fortnite & The Boys coming together for a heroic collaboration, and Fortnite adding AI-Powered NPCs.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: WWE’s Cody Rhodes Crashes CinemaCon Dressed As Street Fighter’s Guile
item.content:encoded:
The upcoming Street Fighter movie made a splash at CinemaCon, thanks in large part to one of its biggest stars. WWE’s Cody Rhodes turned heads as he arrived fully suited up as Guile, the iconic fighter he’s set to portray in the live-action adaptation.

Rhodes was officially unveiled as a cast member four months ago, but his appearance at the event made it clear just how invested he is in bringing the character to life. Dressed in Guile’s signature look, he joined the rest of the cast to promote the film as anticipation continues to build.

New Street Fighter Trailer Coming Very Soon

The cast’s appearance comes just as excitement ramps up for the film’s next major reveal. A new trailer has been teased on social media and is set to release publicly at 11 AM PT, meaning you won’t have to wait much longer to get a fresh look at the movie.

That said, attendees at CinemaCon have already had an early preview—and initial reactions suggest it’s one worth getting excited about. Promotional flyers have also started appearing worldwide, many placed near iconic locations in the game’s classic stages, further fuelling hype.

According to early details, the film will follow Ryu and Ken as they reunite after Chun-Li recruits them to take part in the World Warrior Tournament. However, a deeper conspiracy threatens to tear them apart, forcing the pair to confront their past—or risk total destruction.

With the movie set to release on October 16, 2026, you won’t have to wait much longer until you can see whether this latest video game adaptation lives up to expectations.

Are you excited for the upcoming live-action Street Fighter movie? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the new AI-powered NPCs in Fortnite, as well as the Gears of War: E-Day direct runtime.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Fortnite & The Boys Coming Together For Heroic Collaboration, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
If you’re also devastated that The Boys Season 5 is the end, then don’t worry, according to leaks, the incredible TV Show’s legacy is set to carry on with a presence in Fortnite.

With the MCU dominating Hollywood for years, The Boys was a refreshing self-aware retelling of ‘superheroes’ or ‘supes.’ Based on the comics, The Boys has been one of the biggest streaming hits over the last few years, and it’s finally coming to an end in 2026. A new Fortnite leak claims that the shooter is finally ready to team up, and it would be a crime if they didn’t.

The Boys & Fortnite Crossover Happening

Courtesy of @SamLeakss, one of the many leakers in the Fortnite community, we’ve learned that the two names are colliding.

“THE BOYS x FORTNITE IS CONFIRMED TO BE IN THE WORKS. Will most likely drop later this season, and should also feature car cosmetics.”

In the words of Butcher…Diabolical.

It’s not been revealed yet which characters are getting skins. To be honest, you could give everyone a skin, and they’d sell. Former members of The Seven, Butcher, Hughie, Mother’s Milk, the list goes on—maybe not Translucent.

We’ve previously seen The Boys collab with Modern Warfare 3, and Rainbow 6 Siege was another game to collab with The Boys, so these might give you an idea of what cosmetics to expect.

With so many crossovers and collaborations in the works, there’s no specific timeframe for when The Boys content will drop, but it should be during Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2.

What Fortnite outfits or cosmetics are you desperate to see from The Boys? Are you as gutted as we are that the show is finishing up, despite it still being so good? Share your thoughts on The Boys, Fortnite, the collab, and more through our Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about Fortnite adding AI-Powered NPCs as a new feature and Insomniac reaffirming that more Wolverine news is coming.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Falcons Progress to Semis with Win Over Vitality; IEM Rio 2026 Playoffs Preview
item.content:encoded:
The IEM Rio 2026 Playoff bracket has been set following the last day of Group Stage Counter-Strike action at the Farmasi Arena in Rio de Janeiro.

The last day of the Group Stage came with a significant shock as Falcons defeated Vitality, to progress to straight to the Semi-Finals, meaning Vitality will start their playoffs run on the quarter-finals.

How the last day of the IEM Rio Group Stage progressed

Wednesday was a bumper day, starting with the lower bracket Semis, where G2 and Spirit took down 3DMAX and Red Canids, with Spirit shockingly dropping a map to the Brazilian roster.

In Group B, NAVI eliminated HOTU, and Aurora defeated B8, setting up yet another match between the two rosters, who played the ESL Pro League Season 23 final and in the BLAST Rotterdam groups.

Next up was the Upper Bracket finals, where Falcons shocked Vitality 2-1 in the series. While Vitality kept their Dust 2 record alive, the win ended their series-winning streak, which had been intact since BLAST Bounty when the dominant team lost to Falcons with a stand-in.

In the other group, FURIA cleanly dispatched Mouz 13-5, 13-9 despite Mouz’s clean run to the UB Final. Both rosters have struggled to find their 2025 form, but FURIA seems to be mustering all their might for their home event, advancing directly to the Semi-Finals.

The last two playoff spots were decided with Spirit taking down G2 13-7, 13-11. Later, NAVI once again defeated Aurora 2-1 to maintain their streak against the Turkish roster and progress to the Quarter-Finals.

IEM Rio 2026 Playoff bracket

The IEM Rio 2026 Playoffs will run from Friday April 17 to Sunday April 19, culminating in a Best of 5 Grand Final.

Quarter-finals, April 17:

  • Spirit vs Mouz – 11:00 EDT/17:00 CEST
  • Vitality vs NAVI – 14:15 EDT/20:15 CEST

Semi-Finals, April 18:

  • Falcons vs winner Spirit/Mouz – 11:00 EDT/17:00 CEST
  • FURIA vs winner of Vitality/NAVI – 14:15 EDT/20:15 CEST

Third-place decider:

  • Loser of SF1 vs Loser of SF2 – 08:30 EDT/14:30 CEST

Grand Final:

  • Winner of SF1 vs Winner of SF2 – 13:00 EDT/19:00 CEST

IEM Rio 2026 Playoffs Preview

IEM Rio 2026 is one of the most scintillating brackets in a while, not least because Vitality looks vulnerable at the event. After not dropping 24 maps in a row and 18 series in a row, both streaks have ended at Rio.

Vitality seems to be somewhat shrinking under the pressure of winning the ESL Grand Slam at this event, but a Vitality under pressure is still an elite team due to their sky-high floor.

Falcons, on the other hand, seem completely free of pressure, not in small part because of the rumored roster change of Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen replacing Damjan ‘kyxsan’ Stoilkovski.

We’ve seen that Falcons play their best in those conditions, such as BLAST Bounty when playing with a stand-in due to Maksim ‘kyousuke’ Lukin’s Visa issues. In that instance, once the Grand Final and glory loomed, the pressure returned, and they fell 0-3 to Parivision.

Falcons have shown that their individual level can challenge Vitality under certain conditions, but there’s a lot of CS to be played before we (potentially) get that rematch.

The Playoffs will begin with Mouz and Spirit, two teams that have somewhat fallen apart under the weight of chasing Vitality.

Spirit has looked abject at times at this event, still lacking any level of tactical depth, and deprived of the coach. Danil ‘donk’ Kryshkovets suffered a career low rating of 0.30 against Falcons on Mirage in their series defeat.

Donk later clarified on Telegram that he was feeling unwell at the event, but they’ll need him at his best in playoffs to make any progress.

MOUZ looked more like themselves after a poor exit at ESL Pro League Season 23 was followed by a last-place finish at BLAST Rotterdam. Wins against Legacy and Aurora were somewhat dampened by a devastating loss to Furia, who blew MOUZ out of the server wholesale.

With Falcons waiting in the Semis, a Semi-Final berth seems possible for MOUZ if they can recapture their usual stable level, but going beyond that seems unlikely when they look so vulnerable in comparison to 2025.

For Vitality, the path will really test whether they can return to form. A quarter-final against NAVI would have to be followed by a battle against home favourites FURIA.

FURIA have returned to a decent level in Rio. It seems the roster has been well prepared for the event, wanting to put on a good show for their home fans.

That is not unequivocal; however, the roster was within a whisker of losing to NAVI in the Group Stages, still looking far from the late-2025 level that saw them capture four trophies.

But when 15,000 Brazilian fans are wildly cheering your every kill on, one hopes that FURIA can use that energy and momentum to give anyone a challenge.

Who do you think will win IEM Rio 2026? Let us know on our Discord!
For more CS news, see where stavn has gone in his return to pro play.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 16, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Animal
  • Hint 2: Can be a pet
  • Hint 3: Similar to a weasel

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Dog
  • Kitten
  • Rodent
  • Guinea
  • Pet
  • Cat
  • Rabbit
  • Hamster
  • Chinchilla

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 16, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Ferret

A Ferret is a small mammal with a long body, bred for hunting rabbits and other small animals.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 17.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up? Fortnite April 16 Downtime, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite is still in the midst of Chapter 7, Season 2, but major changes are coming to various game modes in the April 16 update, which has left Fortnite servers unavailable for a period of time.

Epic Games deploys Fortnite updates every two weeks on average, and, following on from the April Fool’s update, a fresh version of the game is being deployed today.

If you’re itching for your Fortnite fix and want to know when the servers will be available again, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

Fortnite servers are down for several hours for the April 16 update, although the exact time they will return has not been confirmed by Epic Games.

Downtime on April 16, which marks the start of a new Fortnite Festival Season and marks the end of Fortnite Ballistic and Festival Battle Stage, begins at 4 AM ET. While the exact length of downtime has not been shared, previous experience provides an expected time frame.

  • More Insider Gaming: Stone Cold Steve Austin Comes to Fortnite This Week

For mid-season updates, downtime is not as long as the seasonal transitions, like the move to a new Chapter, and, instead, downtime lasts for around three to four hours on average.

Using that metric, the Fortnite servers should be back up around 7 AM to 8 AM ET on April 16, although this is subject to change, particularly if any issues are discovered while the patch is deployed.

What’s In The Fortnite April 16 Update?

The headline changes in the Fortnite April 16 update is the start of a new Fortnite Festival season, alongside the permanent removal of Festival Battle Stage and Fortnite Ballistic.

The removal of Battle Stage and Ballistic was announced last month, alongside Rocket Racing, although the latter has been given a reprieve and will be available to play for significantly longer.

  • More Insider Gaming: Sony Sued Over ‘Bye Bye Bye’ Dance in Deapool & Fortnite

Meanwhile, Laufey is the headline act for the new Fortnite Festival season, while the standard Battle Royale will see the second act of Showdown, including a new Infinity Blade.

Additionally, a new Karaoke Festival mode is landing, alongside Save The World’s move to free-to-play, and new collabs will arrive for WWE superstars Liv Morgan and Stone Cold Steve Austin.

What are you most excited to see in the latest Fortnite update? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, read about seven new game modes leaking, and take a look at when changes to Fortnite Crew come into effect.
